commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=b037682c72571ded4898c1bdf5b5b676512d6c53)
commands.review_summary:
The PR reorders legacy payload receipt kind inference and adds a regression test so payloads containing both text and presentation classify as card instead of text.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +22. Total +22 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows the reported Slack path can still fall back to text because core needs `handler.renderPresentation`, while the active Slack facade does not expose Slack's existing renderer.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs a code change for the Slack facade bridge plus contributor-visible real behavior proof, so it is not a safe automated repair-only lane.

target.title: fix(channels): prioritize presentation over text in receipt kind resolution
target.body:
## Summary

In `resolvePayloadReceiptKind` (`src/channels/message/outbound-bridge.ts`), the `text` check was evaluated before the `presentation` check. When a message carries both text and a presentation payload (e.g. Slack Block Kit), the function returned `"text"` and the presentation was never rendered as a card.

Move the `presentation`/`interactive` check above the `text` check so that messages with presentation blocks always resolve as `"card"`.

Fixes #95440

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [ ] Gateway / [ ] Skills / [ ] Auth / [ ] Memory / [ ] Integrations / [x] API / [ ] UI/UX / [ ] CI

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Slack presentation payload (Block Kit) is delivered as plain text (`kind: "text"`) instead of card (`kind: "card"`) when the message also carries text content, because `resolvePayloadReceiptKind` checks text before presentation.

**Environment tested:** Node.js v24.13.1 on Linux, OpenClaw main branch and PR branch.

**Exact steps run after the patch:** Called `createChannelMessageAdapterFromOutbound` with `sendPayload` handler and sent a payload with both `text: "test"` and `presentation: { blocks: [...] }`, then inspected `receipt.parts[0].kind`.

**Evidence before fix (upstream/main — bug present):**

```text
=== #95440 proof ===
Input: text='test' + presentation with blocks
Receipt kind: text
Expected: card
Result: FAIL ❌

Input: text='' + presentation with blocks
Receipt kind: card
Expected: card
Result: PASS ✅

Input: text='hello' only (no presentation)
Receipt kind: text
Expected: text
Result: PASS ✅
```

**Evidence after fix (PR branch — bug fixed):**

```text
=== #95440 proof ===
Input: text='test' + presentation with blocks
Receipt kind: card
Expected: card
Result: PASS ✅

Input: text='' + presentation with blocks
Receipt kind: card
Expected: card
Result: PASS ✅

Input: text='hello' only (no presentation)
Receipt kind: text
Expected: text
Result: PASS ✅
```

**Observed result after the fix:** Messages with both text and presentation now correctly resolve as `"card"`. Messages with presentation only (no text) still resolve as `"card"`. Messages with text only (no presentation) still resolve as `"text"`. No existing behavior is broken.

**What was not tested:** Live Slack delivery with `openclaw message send --presentation` against a real Slack workspace.
